text.title: YAML Frontmatter Validation and Common Pitfalls
text.summary: Common YAML syntax mistakes, CLI validation commands, and CI integration rules for frontmatter.

antipattern.description: Tab characters used for indentation inside frontmatter.
antipattern.reason: YAML strictly requires spaces. A tab character causes a parse error or silently corrupts the block in lenient parsers.

antipattern.description: Comma-separated string used where an array is required: `tags: a, b, c`.
antipattern.reason: This parses as a scalar string, not an array. Use `tags: [a, b, c]` or multiline YAML array syntax.

antipattern.description: Patching only one field with an agent regenerates the entire frontmatter block, erasing `created` and other agent-set fields.
antipattern.reason: Agents asked to "update the status field" often rewrite the full block. Use `yq` for single-field patches to preserve all other fields.

antipattern.description: Nesting frontmatter more than 2 levels deep.
antipattern.reason: Deeply nested frontmatter is difficult to query with standard tools and breaks most SSG schema validators. Move complex data to the document body.

rule.statement: Run frontmatter validation as a pre-commit hook so invalid documents are caught before they enter the repository.
rule.rationale: CI validation costs round-trips; local validation is immediate. Agents committing invalid frontmatter must get blocked pre-commit, not post-push.

example.code:
# Validate a single file with yq yq eval '.' doc.md > /dev/null && echo "OK" || echo "INVALID YAML"
# Check required fields with Python validator python validate-frontmatter.py .aidocs/todo/**/*.md
# Quick syntax-only check python -c "import frontmatter; frontmatter.load('doc.md')" && echo "OK"
example.note: yq is the most reliable single-field patching and validation tool for YAML frontmatter.

rule.statement: Add frontmatter validation to the CI pipeline using `front-matter-schema` GitHub Action or an equivalent validator.
rule.rationale: Zod validation errors in Astro content collections produce build failures, not runtime errors. Agent-generated content must be validated before the build runs.

rule.statement: Add `updated: YYYY-MM-DD` to every document an agent modifies — do not rely on file mtime.
rule.rationale: File modification times are unreliable across git checkouts and CI environments. The `updated` field enables recency sorting by any tool.

text.reference: Python script validating required fields and enum values for SDD doc types
